tweets.content: What's your go-to move when you arrive somewhere new? Wander aimlessly, hit the famous spots first, or ask locals for their secret recommendations?

Spring break planning stress hitting different when your group can't agree on anything? Roamly's private preference collection lets everyone be honest about what they actually want without the awkward group chat drama ✈️ #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

Spring break timing tip: Book that shoulder season Europe trip for late April instead of peak summer. 40% cheaper flights, fewer crowds, and perfect weather. Plus you'll actually get those Instagram shots without 200 tourists in the background 📸 #travel #traveltech

Cherry blossom season in Kyoto is magical but here's the thing nobody tells you: peak bloom lasts maybe 5 days and the crowds are insane. Book for late April instead when petals are falling like snow and you can actually breathe 🌸 #travel #digitalnomad

Roamly's date coordination just saved another group from the "when can everyone go?" spiral that kills 90% of trips before they start. One click finds windows that actually work for everyone 🗓️ https://roamly.vientapps.com #travel #traveltech #buildinpublic

Just pushed a gnarly bug fix that was breaking Roamly's AI planning for groups over 8 people. Turns out I was concatenating preferences wrong and Claude was getting confused. 3am debugging sessions hit different when users are counting on you 🐛 #indiehackers #buildinpublic

The hardest part about building Roamly isn't the AI or the date coordination logic. It's deciding which features NOT to build. Users suggest amazing ideas daily but every yes is a thousand nos to something else #indiehackers #buildinpublic

Building a YouTube Short generator taught me that AI tools work best when you treat them like specialized contractors, not magic boxes. Claude writes scripts, Fal generates images, ElevenLabs does voiceover. Each does one thing well. #ai #buildinpublic #devlog

Porto's port wine cellars are caves carved into cliffsides where 200-year-old barrels age in darkness. Sip vintage tawny watching sunset paint the Douro gold. Spring = fewer crowds, perfect weather ⚡ #travel #buildinpublic #portugal

When you rebuild a client's site from scratch after an AI builder fails them, do you eat the extra hours or charge for the lesson learned? Asking for a friend who just spent 20 hours undoing vendor lock-in 😅 #webdev #indiehackers #clientwork

Roamly's no-go zones feature hits different for spring break. Tell it "skip party destinations" and watch it craft perfect alternatives. Your group gets adventure without the chaos they didn't want 🚫 #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

Paper trading my Trump Truth Social bot worked flawlessly. Real markets? Different story. Turns out the 3-second delay between RSS feed → Claude analysis → trade execution meant I was always buying the top of moves that lasted 30 seconds 📉 #ai #trading #devlog

Just shipped PackSmart: free AI packing tool that checks real weather for your dates then tells you what to bring. No more jacket anxiety 🎒 https://vientapps.com/blog/building-packsmart #buildinpublic #ai #travel

Ever shipped something simple that unexpectedly took off? My joke extension started as "I want a clean joke in new tabs" and somehow 4,600 people installed it. What's your accidental success story? #indiehackers #chromeextension #buildinpublic

Roamly's style preferences are saving spring break groups from the "beach vs culture" death spiral. Tag yourself as Nature + Active Sports, your friend picks Beach + Nightlife, and AI builds days that actually work for everyone 🌿 #travel #buildinpublic #traveltech

jsPDF for media kit generation was the wrong choice. 60-second render times, layout nightmares, and memory leaks with images. Should've gone with Puppeteer + HTML/CSS from day one. Sometimes the boring solution is the right one 🤦‍♂️ #webdev #typescript #devlog

Mexico City's street food runs deeper than tacos. Hunt down esquites in Roma Norte, then follow locals to hole-in-wall pulquerías where fermented agave flows. Spring weather = perfect wandering without melting 🌶️ #travel #buildinpublic #mexico

Charleston's real magic hides down cobblestone alleys where 300-year-old houses lean together, whispering secrets. Spring brings jasmine evenings and porch swings creaking with stories older than America 🏛️ #travel #buildinpublic #charleston

Claude Code's `/usage` and `/context` break flow when you check them constantly. Built a bash statusline showing rate limits, context window, and reset timer as progress bars on every prompt. One glance = full picture 📊 #buildinpublic #claude #bash

Most SEO tools check if Google can crawl you. None check if ChatGPT can find you when users ask about your product. Built ShipReady to audit for answer engines. Check if your robots.txt blocks GPTBot right now https://vientapps.com/blog/building-shipready #seo #ai #buildinpublic

The 3-Hour Rule: If your multiplayer game idea takes more than 3 hours to explain the technical architecture, you're overthinking it. Firebase arrays, real-time sync, turn management - keep it simple or players bounce before the fun starts #gamedev #reactnative #buildinpublic
AI builders get you 80% there in minutes, but fixing that last 20% costs more than building from scratch. Spent 6 hours undoing LandingSite.ai's mess, rebuilt clean in 2 with Astro.

Claude Code on Bedrock blocks microphone access for security but I type at 20 WPM. Built a dead simple iOS app: tap button, speak, text hits clipboard instantly. One paste vs minutes of hunting-and-pecking #buildinpublic #reactnative #speechrecognition

BurnRate worked perfectly until I realized the API keys it needed could drain your account with one bad call. Security > convenience. Build the simple version first: spreadsheet tracking https://vientapps.com/blog/shelving-burnrate #buildinpublic #indiehackers #api

Carrier-specific bag limits aren't edge cases - they're the problem. Built size checkers with orientation-agnostic math: your 22x14x9 bag fits Delta vertically but Ryanair horizontally. One JSON, 50 airlines, zero €55 surprises #typescript #travel #buildinpublic

The Preference Paralysis Rule: Groups debating in chat take 3x longer to book and pick destinations 40% less satisfying. Collect votes privately first, discuss only top 3. Democracy works better in darkness #indiehackers #buildinpublic #travel

The 6-Decision Rule: When stuck on design direction, give AI exactly 6 "decide for me" constraints and ship whatever it builds. Claude turned my static homepage into animated flight paths in one conversation.

The Forbes Mention Rule: When press validates your indie tools, screenshot but wait 24 hours before posting. Dopamine clouds judgment. Share the technical win, not the ego boost. Build tools people cite, not clout #indiehackers #buildinpublic #press

iframe widgets seem simple until airlines change their bag rules and your embed breaks on 500 travel blogs. Built the carry-on checker with JSON configs so one update fixes everywhere. Separation of data from display = fewer 3am panic fixes 🎒 #webdev #astro #buildinpublic

When you build a travel widget, do you optimize for the 80% case (basic fees) or the 20% edge case (overweight surcharges, third bags, size limits)? My checked bag calculator handles all of it - curious what tradeoffs you make in your tools? #webdev #buildinpublic #travel

Most devs skip locale-aware date parsing until users start complaining about "invalid date" errors. `new Date('2026-04-30')` breaks in different timezones. Use `date-fns/parseISO` instead - handles ISO strings consistently everywhere #webdev #javascript #buildinpublic

Bali's rice terraces cascade down volcanic slopes in emerald steps carved over 1,000 years. Trek Jatiluwih at sunrise when mist clings to ancient channels. May = dry season magic without July crowds 🌾 #travel #buildinpublic #bali

What's the most annoying layover experience you've had because of bad terminal transfer info? I just built a calculator that knows every JFK terminal-to-terminal walk time after getting burned by a 45-minute "5-minute" connection myself #travel #webdev #buildinpublic

Ever been surprised by a gate agent rejecting your "personal item" when your carry-on sailed through? I analyzed 75 airlines and found 32% don't even publish personal item dimensions. What's your worst gate-check story? #travel #indiehackers #buildinpublic

When you're building a puzzle generator, what's harder: creating the algorithm that makes valid puzzles or the one that makes puzzles actually fun to solve? Spent 3 weeks on SumTrails learning they're totally different problems #gamedev #reactnative #buildinpublic

The 90-Minute Rule: Book layovers with 90+ minutes domestic, 2+ hours international. That $80 cheaper tight connection costs $200+ when you miss it. Learned this sprinting through Dallas with 55 minutes 🏃‍♂️ #travel #buildinpublic #indiehackers

Flying with pets: $300 on United vs $74 on WestJet for identical under-seat service. 4x price difference, zero service difference. Check pet fees before booking, not at checkout https://vientapps.com/blog/21-airline-pet-fees-74-to-300-spread #travel #indiehackers

Complex state in React Native games? Skip Redux/MobX. Use one useRef with immutable updates: `gameRef.current = {...gameRef.current, score}`. Zero re-renders, predictable state #gamedev #reactnative #buildinpublic

15 countries accept US plugs but run 230V that'll fry your 110V hair dryer. Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines look "compatible" until you smell burning electronics. Check voltage labels first https://vientapps.com/blog/15-countries-us-plug-fits-voltage-doesnt #travel #buildinpublic

When you're debugging travel gear failures in the field, what teaches you more: the thing that breaks or figuring out why the backup also failed? Just traced 15 countries where US plugs fit but voltage fries your gear #travel #buildinpublic #devlog

The Zero Assets Rule: When clients have literally nothing (no photos, no copy, no branding), charge 3x your normal rate. Building from absolute zero means you're doing design, copywriting, photography direction, and dev. That's 4 jobs, not 1 #webdev #clientwork #indiehackers

Roamly's no-go zones prevent those awkward "I hate crowds" vs "I need nightlife" battles. Mark Times Square as a hard no, your party animal friend tags club districts as must-haves, and AI builds NYC days where everyone's actually happy 🗽 #travel #ai #buildinpublic

The Automation Paradox: Spent 6 hours building a WoW addon to save 5 seconds per portal request. Classic programmer move - solve a 30-second problem with 3 days of Lua wrestling. Sometimes inefficiency is the whole point #gamedev #lua #buildinpublic

Paper trading an AI bot that reads Truth Social? Perfect results. Real markets with 200ms latency? Lost money faster than a JavaScript memory leak. Rule: backtest with real API delays, not localhost dreams #python #trading #buildinpublic
AI builders promise "full control" until you need custom schema markup. LandingSite handled meta tags but locked me out of local SEO. Rebuilt Griffin Renovation in vanilla HTML + Tailwind in 2 hours.

When building AI tools that need to output structured data, do you validate the JSON before using it or just pray the LLM followed instructions? PackSmart taught me hard lessons about Claude occasionally forgetting closing brackets mid-packing list 🎒 #webdev #ai #buildinpublic

Most subscription tools solve fake problems. Creator needed a media kit PDF, losing brand deals. Built CreatorKit: free, no signup, 60-second generation. Zero usage = shelved https://vientapps.com/blog/building-creatorkit #buildinpublic #indiehackers

The 8.5x Cost Rule: Maui at $170/day vs Ho Chi Minh City at $20/day. Same week of vacation budget = 1 week luxury beach or 8.5 weeks exploring Southeast Asia. Pick your trade-off: Instagram stories or life-changing memories #travel #buildinpublic #indiehackers

Tbilisi's sulfur baths sit in caves carved into cliff faces where locals have soaked for 1,500 years. The water hits your skin at exactly 37°C from natural springs. May = perfect weather before summer heat kicks in 🛁 #travel #georgia #buildinpublic

Most devs think "automated content" means spam. I built a YouTube Shorts pipeline: text script → finished video using Claude + FFmpeg. Weekend project, real costs https://vientapps.com/blog/generating-youtube-shorts-with-ai #ai #typescript #buildinpublic
Lighthouse gives perfect SEO scores while ChatGPT has never heard of your site. Built ShipReady to audit what matters: GPTBot access, llms.txt, answer engine structure.

The 60-Second Visibility Rule: Your Claude Code session limits are invisible until they hit. Built /statusline to show usage bars, context window, and 5-hour reset timer in every prompt. One bash command saves hours of surprise cooldowns #buildinpublic #claude #devlog

When you need a client site live in hours, do you reach for AI builders like LandingSite or stick with hand-coded solutions? Just shipped Griffin Renovation in an afternoon but hit real limits when they wanted custom schema markup #webdev #ai #buildinpublic

Voice transcription isn't "AI magic" until you need it working offline with 99% accuracy. Built Mic to Clipboard with iOS Speech Framework - zero API calls, instant results. `SFSpeechRecognizer().recognitionTask()` beats cloud services every time #reactnative #ios #buildinpublic

Best Carry-On Bags in 2026 🎒
Carry-on rules got stricter, airline fees climbed higher, and one-bag travel became the norm for smart travelers.
We tested 8 top options on durability, weight, and real-world packing:
Top picks:
1. Peak Design Travel Backpack 45L

---

2. Travelpro Platinum Elite 22" Spinner
3. Monos Carry-On
4. Aer Travel Pack 3
5. Tom Bihn Aeronaut 45

---

Quick take: Peak Design for photographers who commute, Travelpro for frequent flyers wanting reliability, Tom Bihn for lifetime durability. Which style works better for your trips: backpack or wheels? #travel #carryon #packinglight https://travelvient.com/guides/best-carry-on-bags

Frontier cut checked bags to 40 lb in 2026 ✈️

Tightest weight cap of any major US airline, plus $99 gate fees for carry-ons make this tricky budget territory.

We tested the options on cost, flexibility, and hidden fees:

Top picks:
1. Frontier Standard
2. GoWild! Pass

UK261 gets you £220 to £520 cash when flights from UK airports arrive 3+ hours late ✈️ Same rules as EU261 but in pounds, enforced by CAA after Brexit.

---

Amounts by distance:
-> £220 for flights ≤1,500 km
-> £350 for 1,500-3,500 km
-> £260 (3-4h late) or £520 (4h+ late) for long-haul
Weather and ATC strikes don't count.

---

You have 6 years to claim directly from airlines at zero cost. Which delayed flight have you never claimed compensation for? #travel #traveltips #airtravel https://travelvient.com/guides/uk261-flight-compensation

Best airlines for flying while pregnant ✈️
We researched 8 carriers on medical requirements, cutoffs & comfort. Top 3: Delta, JetBlue, American.
Full ranked list 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/best-airline-for-flying-pregnant
#travel #familytravel #traveltips

EU flights 3+ hours late = €250 to €600 cash compensation ✈️ Distance sets the amount: €250 (short), €400 (medium), €600 (long). Any EU departure counts, plus EU airlines flying in. Claim yourself, skip the 30% fees. Full guide 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/eu261-flight-compensation #travel #traveltips

SEA Airport to Seattle cruise terminals is 14 miles, 30-60 minutes depending on I-5 traffic 🚢 Rideshare costs $35-$55, cruise line buses $30-$40. Light rail + taxi combo saves money at $18 total. Full guide 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/sea-to-port-of-seattle #cruise #travel

JetBlue Blue Basic includes full carry-on again as of Sept 2024 ✈️
We ranked all JetBlue fares on value and restrictions. Top picks: Blue Basic (cheapest), Blue Plus (free checked bag), Mint (lie-flat).
Full ranked list 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/jetblue-blue-basic-2026 #travel #airtravel

US airlines don't pay EU-style delay compensation ✈️ But you get automatic full refunds when flights are cancelled or changed 3+ hours domestic/6+ hours international. Refunds due in 7 business days for credit cards. Full guide 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/us-flight-refund-rights-dot #travel #traveltips

Best airlines for flying with musical instruments 🎼
We ranked 8 carriers on policies, fees & musician reports. Top picks: Southwest (guitars), American/Delta (cellos), Air France (cheapest international).
Full ranked list 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/best-airline-for-checked-instruments #travel #airtravel

Krakow's medieval core survived WWII intact while Warsaw was rebuilt from photos 🏰 Main Market Square has been trading since 1257. Budget travelers pay just $40/day. Skip Euronet ATMs, use bank ATMs for zloty. Full guide 👉 https://travelvient.com/destinations/krakow #travel #budgettravel

We ranked Spirit's former fares plus current ULCC alternatives. Top picks now: Frontier, Allegiant, Breeze for ex-Spirit flyers.
Full ranked list 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/spirit-basic-economy-2026 #travel #budgettravel

We tested 8 carriers on fees, size limits & board policies. Top 3: Alaska Airlines, Hawaiian Airlines, United (California hack).

Full ranked list 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/best-esim-for-japan-2026
#travel #digitalnomad #travelhacks

Ships over 500 passengers can't land in Antarctica at all 🚢 Lindblad Expeditions-National Geographic, Hurtigruten Expeditions, Silversea Expeditions. IAATO rules mean only small expedition ships actually get you ashore, not scenic cruising past icebergs #cruise #travel

IAH's published 30-minute domestic connection sounds tight but works ✈️ United's Skyway train connects all 5 terminals behind security in 7 minutes. The trick: use the above-ground Skyway for connections, not the below-ground Subway which is outside security. #travel #airtravel

Manchester Airport became 2-terminal in March 2026 after decades as 3-terminal ✈️ Terminal 2 handles 75%+ of flights with airside connections, but Ryanair stays in T3 with no direct transfer. Walk takes 5-15 min landside. Have you been caught by the new layout? #travel #airtravel

Major airlines all hit $45 for first checked bags after April 2026 hikes 💰 Best calculators: Travel Vient (75+ airlines, free), Google Flights bags filter, KAYAK assistant. Skip single-airline tools that hide competitor pricing. #travel #traveltips #airtravel

Gave Claude Design full creative control ("decide for me" on all 6 questions). It built a 3-canvas world map: 23 rotated ellipses for continents, quadratic Bezier flight arcs, prefers-reduced-motion baked in. Lesson: constraints beat blank-slate freedom, and budget a second se...

Built a TypeScript CLI that turns a text script into a finished YouTube Short for $0.30-0.60. Claude splits scenes, Fal/Kling generates video, ElevenLabs narrates, Whisper syncs subs, FFmpeg assembles. Biggest trap: brew FFmpeg ships without libass. #buildinpublic

Biggest gotcha deploying Next.js 15 to Cloudflare Workers: process.env is silently empty without the nodejs_compat_populate_process_env flag. Also need OpenNext adapter + wrangler secret put. Three separate things, all required, none obvious. #buildinpublic

App Store paperwork ate a full day more than the actual build. Privacy manifest, encryption declaration, permission strings, screenshots. Lesson: take screenshots during dev, not at submission. Did App Store review kill your launch momentum too? #buildinpublic

Prague in summer hits 22-25°C but Old Town turns into a selfie-stick traffic jam 😬 Budget $50/day if you eat in Zizkov, not tourist squares. Avoid Euronet ATMs (huge fees); use Ceska Sporitelna. Tram 22 = free sightseeing for 39 CZK. #travel #budgettravel #traveltips

Built a WoW Classic addon to auto-invite portal buyers. Hardest part wasn't invites, it was pattern matching: Lua has no word boundaries, so %f[%a]port%f[%A] stops 'airport' firing. Trade chat defaults off; too many false positives. Full breakdown 👉 https://vient.org/blog/building-mage-portals

Opened Bloons TD, closed it before playing because 6 modals blocked the map. Now I only build one-feature apps: no accounts, no settings, no upsells. Ships in a weekend, zero onboarding, tiny bug surface. Add feature 2 when feature 1 stops being enough. #buildinpublic

3 gear picks every parent flying with a baby needs this summer 👶

1. CARES Harness, FAA-approved, beats a lap hold
2. Ergobaby Omni Breeze, hands free at security
3. Babyzen YOYO2, fits in the overhead bin

#travel #familytravel

🚢 Flying into Galveston for a cruise? HOU beats IAH every time: 25 miles closer, 30-45 min faster. Rideshare from IAH runs $75-110; shared shuttles (Galveston Limousine, Island Breeze) cost $25-40/person. Did you miss a sail-away because of airport traffic? #cruise #traveltips

🚢 Carnival runs 30-50% cheaper than Royal Caribbean for the same cabin size. Royal Caribbean wins on activities (FlowRider, CocoCay). Disney is worth the premium only for kids under 10. Full breakdown 👉 https://travelvient.com/guides/best-cruise-line-for-families #cruise #familytravel

First version of my travel adapter tool dumped all 15 plug types and voltage data at once. Unusable. Fix: bold verdict first, details behind a collapsible. Claude one-shotted the 221-country JSON. Hierarchy before code, always.

4,600 installs, zero marketing. Just an SEO-optimized Chrome Web Store listing.  MV3 gotcha: call ExtensionPay's startBackground() at the top level of your service worker, not only in onInstalled. Workers are ephemeral; restarts skip onInstalled entirely.
